key_insight: Sliding-window attention (SWA) can become competitive for math reasoning when converted from a pretrained self-attention model and then adapted with reinforcement learning, which reduces the performance gap caused by architecture mismatch.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to long-context reasoning systems with lower attention cost, preserving much of the accuracy of self-attention while moving toward linear-complexity inference.
method: Convert a pretrained self-attention LLM to SWA and apply supervised fine-tuning.
Use on-policy reinforcement learning so the model adapts its own trajectories under the SWA constraint.
Align training data and model architecture to reduce long-range dependency mismatch.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluations on mathematical reasoning benchmarks showing RL substantially narrows the SWA-vs-SA gap and recovers much of the accuracy lost after conversion.
watch_item: The approach may still depend on careful RL stabilization and may not fully solve tasks with strong long-range dependencies beyond the sliding window.
paper_title: Architecture-Aware Reinforcement Learning Makes Sliding-Window Attention Competitive in Math Reasoning
